devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति सन्दिश्य भगवान् बार्हिषदैरभिपूजितः । पश्यतां राजपुत्राणां तत्रैवान्तर्दधे हरः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca iti sandiśya bhagavān bārhiṣadairabhipūjitaḥ paśyatāṃ rājaputrāṇāṃ tatraivāntardadhe haraḥ

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having thus instructed Pracetasas, the sons of Barhiṣad, Lord Śiva who was devoutly worshipped by them, immediately disappeared at that very spot, while the Princes were simply looking on.

devanagari: रुद्रगीतं भगवतः स्तोत्रं सर्वे प्रचेतसः । जपन्तस्ते तपस्तेपुः वर्षाणां अयुतं जले ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: rudragītaṃ bhagavataḥ stotraṃ sarve pracetasaḥ japantaste tapastepuḥ varṣāṇāṃ ayutaṃ jale

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jap class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jap class 1 verb]

translation: Repeating the hymn in praise of the venerable Lord, as sung by Rudra, all the Pracetasa princes standing in water, practised penance for ten thousand years.

devanagari: प्राचीनबर्हिषं क्षत्तः कर्मस्वासक्तमानसम् । नारदोऽध्यात्मतत्त्वज्ञः कृपालुः प्रत्यबोधयत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: prācīnabarhiṣaṃ kṣattaḥ karmasvāsaktamānasam nārado
translation: Oh Vidura! In the meanwhile (while Pracetasas were undergoing austere penance) the compassionate Nārada who knew the reality about the Supreme Soul, (visited and) reasoned with (their father) Prācīnabarhis who was attached to and was preoccupied in ritualistic Vedic acts, (as follows):

devanagari: श्रेयस्त्वं कतमद्राजन् कर्मणात्मन ईहसे । दुःखहानिः सुखावाप्तिः श्रेयस्तन्नेह चेष्यते ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śreyastvaṃ katamadrājan karmaṇātmana īhase duḥkhahāniḥ sukhāvāptiḥ śreyastanneha ceṣyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active second single], [aorist active third single]
[aorist active second single], [aorist active third single]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single], [aorist active second single], [aorist active third single]

[nominative single from √ śrai class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ śrai class 4 verb]

translation: “Oh King! What bliss do you attempt to secure by the performance of ritualistic acts? That which removes miseries and secures happiness is (called the real) bliss. And (according to the wise) such beatitude is not expected from the karmas.”

devanagari: राजोवाच । न जानामि महाभाग परं कर्मापविद्धधीः । ब्रूहि मे विमलं ज्ञानं येन मुच्येय कर्मभिः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: rājovāca na jānāmi mahābhāga paraṃ karmāpaviddhadhīḥ brūhi me vimalaṃ jñānaṃ yena mucyeya karmabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King said:
“Oh blessed Sir! My mind is distracted by the ritualistic karmas. I do not know the supreme bliss. Please initiate me in that pure knowledge whereby I can be free from (bonds of all) karmas.

devanagari: गृहेषु कूटधर्मेषु पुत्रदारधनार्थधीः । न परं विन्दते मूढो भ्राम्यन् संसारवर्त्मसु ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛheṣu kūṭadharmeṣu putradāradhanārthadhīḥ na paraṃ vindate mūḍho bhrāmyan saṃsāravartmasu
translation: In the householder’s life which is characterised by deceitfulness, a person looks upon his sons, wife and wealth as the sole objectives in life. Such ignorant persons, wandering in the pathways of saṃsāra (transmigration of the Soul), never attain the Supreme Bliss.

devanagari: नारद उवाच । भो भोः प्रजापते राजन् पशून् पश्य त्वयाध्वरे । संज्ञापिताञ्जीवसङ्घान् निर्घृणेन सहस्रशः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvāca bho bhoḥ prajāpate rājan paśūn paśya tvayādhvare saṃjñāpitāñjīvasaṅghān nirghṛṇena sahasraśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Nārada said[1]:
Oh Lord of men! Have a look at those multitudes of living beings mercilessly slaughtered by you in thousands, as beasts (consecrated) for sacrifice.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
By his Yogic powers Nārada showed to the King the huge multitudes of beasts slaughtered by him for sacrifice—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: एते त्वां सम्प्रतीक्षन्ते स्मरन्तो वैशसं तव । सम्परेतमयःकूटैः छिन्दति उत्थितमन्यवः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ete tvāṃ sampratīkṣante smaranto vaiśasaṃ tava samparetamayaḥkūṭaiḥ chindati utthitamanyavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Remembering your cruelty (to them), they are waiting for you. When you will be dead, they, with their ire aroused, will pierce you (through and through) with their steellike horns.

devanagari: अत्र ते कथयिष्येऽमुं इतिहासं पुरातनम् । पुरञ्जनस्य चरितं निबोध गदतो मम ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: atra te kathayiṣye purañjanasya caritaṃ nibodha gadato mama
translation: On this point, I shall narrate to you this ancient legend. Listen to the story of Purañjana as I tell you.

devanagari: आसीत्पुरञ्जनो नाम राजा राजन् बृहच्छ्रवाः । तस्याविज्ञातनामाऽसीत् सखाविज्ञातचेष्टितः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: āsītpurañjano nāma rājā rājan bṛhacchravāḥ tasyāvijñātanāmā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh King, there was a king of wide fame, by name Purañjana (i.e. Jīva). He had a friend called Avijñāta (i.e. the Supreme Soul) as his activities were unknown.

devanagari: सोऽन्वेषमाणः शरणं बभ्राम पृथिवीं प्रभुः । नानुरूपं यदाविन्दद् अभूत्स विमना इव ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: so nānurūpaṃ yadāvindad abhūtsa vimanā iva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ ind class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ind class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ind class 1 verb]

translation: The king wandered over the earth[2] in search of an abode.[3] When he did not find any suitable asylum, he became rather dejected.[4]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Pṛthivī—This indicates the universe—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā karma-bhumī—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [3]:
Śaraṇam [Śaraṇa]—Body through which the jīva gets experience in life.

Footnote [4]:
Although he (the jīva) gets sensual pleasures in all births, he is not satisfied as non-attachment, which is the means of the Liberation (Mokṣa) is not possible in non-human births. Hence his dejection—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: न साधु मेने ताः सर्वा भूतले यावतीः पुरः । कामान् कामयमानोऽसौ तस्य तस्योपपत्तये ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: na sādhu mene tāḥ sarvā bhūtale yāvatīḥ puraḥ kāmān kāmayamāno

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Seeking after pleasures, he did not approve of any of the cities that existed on the earth as suitable for the realization of those pleasures.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
There is no possibility of enjoying all the earthly and heavenly pleasures in non-human species. Hence his disapproval—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā Gf. tābhyo gām ānayat tā abruvan na vai no'yam alam etc.—Ait. Up., 2.2.

devanagari: स एकदा हिमवतो दक्षिणेष्वथ सानुषु । ददर्श नवभिर्द्वार्भिः पुरं लक्षितलक्षणाम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ekadā himavato dakṣiṇeṣvatha sānuṣu dadarśa navabhirdvārbhiḥ puraṃ lakṣitalakṣaṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: [6] Once upon a time, he saw, on the table land in the southern range of the Himālayas, a city having nine gates and possessing all the characteristics of a good city.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
In Bhāratavarṣa, to the south of the Himālayas, he saw the city (human body) which was free from disabilities and handicaps like blindness, deafness—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: प्राकारोपवनाट्टाल परिखैरक्षतोरणैः । स्वर्णरौप्यायसैः शृङ्गैः सङ्कुलां सर्वतो गृहैः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: prākāropavanāṭṭāla parikhairakṣatoraṇaiḥ svarṇaraupyāyasaiḥ śṛṅgaiḥ saṅkulāṃ sarvato gṛhaiḥ
translation: [7] It was surrounded by ramparts, gardens, watchtowers, moats, windows, arched gateways. It was crowded on all sides with houses with crests of gold, silver and iron.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
The limbs or sense-organs of the body are the ramparts etc. The plexuses (cakras like ādhāra etc.) are the houses. The three types of turrets or house-crests are the guṇas viz. sattva, rajas and tamas—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: नीलस्फटिकवैदूर्य मुक्तामरकतारुणैः । कॢप्तहर्म्यस्थलीं दीप्तां श्रिया भोगवतीमिव ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nīlasphaṭikavaidūrya muktāmarakatāruṇaiḥ kḷptaharmyasthalīṃ dīptāṃ śriyā bhogavatīmiva
translation: It appeared resplendent like Bhogavatī,[8] in beauty, with floors of its mansions studded with precious stones like sapphires, crystals, cat’s eyes, pearls, emeralds and rubies.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Bhogavatī: Literally—a place for enjoyment of pleasures, but it is generally derived from bhoga ‘serpent’s body’. It is the name of the city of snakes in the lower-regions, famous for its beauty.

devanagari: सभाचत्वर रथ्याभिः आक्रीडायतनापणैः । चैत्यध्वजपताकाभिः युक्तां विद्रुमवेदिभिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhācatvara rathyābhiḥ ākrīḍāyatanāpaṇaiḥ caityadhvajapatākābhiḥ yuktāṃ vidrumavedibhiḥ
translation: It appeared beautiful with assembly halls, courtyards, main roads (highways), gambling houses, markets, resthouses, flags and fluttering banners and platforms built of coral.

devanagari: पुर्यास्तु बाह्योपवने दिव्यद्रुमलताकुले । नदद् विहङ्गालिकुल कोलाहलजलाशये ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: puryāstu bāhyopavane divyadrumalatākule nadad vihaṅgālikula kolāhalajalāśaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nad class 1 verb]

translation: Outside the city, there was a park full of celestial trees and creepers. There was a lake resounding with warbling sounds of birds and humming of bees.

devanagari: हिमनिर्झरविप्रुष्मत् कुसुमाकरवायुना । चलत्प्रवालविटप नलिनीतटसम्पदि ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: himanirjharavipruṣmat kusumākaravāyunā calatpravālaviṭapa nalinītaṭasampadi
translation: The bank of the lotus pond was richly beautified with trees the foliage and branches of which were fluttering with the cool breeze laden with spray from the cascades and blowing over the flower beds.

devanagari: नानारण्यमृगव्रातैः अनाबाधे मुनिव्रतैः । आहूतं मन्यते पान्थो यत्र कोकिलकूजितैः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: nānāraṇyamṛgavrātaiḥ anābādhe munivrataiḥ āhūtaṃ manyate pāntho yatra kokilakūjitaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: It was safe and free from molestation as the multitudes of wild animals infesting it were observing ascetic vows (e.g. non-violence etc.). The traveller feels that he is invited there, by the cooing of the cuckoos.

devanagari: यदृच्छयागतां तत्र ददर्श प्रमदोत्तमाम् । भृत्यैर्दशभिरायान्तीं एकैकशतनायकैः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yadṛcchayāgatāṃ tatra dadarśa pramadottamām bhṛtyairdaśabhirāyāntīṃ ekaikaśatanāyakaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: [9] In that garden, he (King Purañjana) saw a young damsel of superb beauty coming there by chance. She was accompanied by ten servants each of whom was the husband of hundred ladies.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
The lady is the intelligence (buddhi) and the ten attendants are the conative ana cognitive sense-organs and the hundred (i.e. hundreds of) activities or vṛttis of each are the wives.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: अञ्चशीर्षाहिना गुप्तां प्रतीहारेण सर्वतः । अन्वेषमाणां ऋषभं अप्रौढां कामरूपिणीम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: añcaśīrṣāhinā guptāṃ pratīhāreṇa sarvataḥ anveṣamāṇāṃ ṛṣabhaṃ aprauḍhāṃ kāmarūpiṇīm

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: She was guarded on all sides by her bodyguard, a serpent with five hoods.[10] She was sixteen years of age, beautiful or capable of assuming any form she liked. She was in search of a husband.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
This is Prāṇa of five vital functions—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: सुनासां सुदतीं बालां सुकपोलां वराननाम् । समविन्यस्तकर्णाभ्यां बिभ्रतीं कुण्डलश्रियम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: sunāsāṃ sudatīṃ bālāṃ sukapolāṃ varānanām samavinyastakarṇābhyāṃ bibhratīṃ kuṇḍalaśriyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural], [locative single]

translation: The girl had a shapely nose, beautiful rows of teeth, lovely cheeks, attractive face and was wearing lustrous earrings in her symmetrical ears.

devanagari: पिशङ्गनीवीं सुश्रोणीं श्यामां कनकमेखलाम् । पद्‍भ्यां क्वणद्‍भ्यां चलन्तीं नूपुरैर्देवतामिव ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: piśaṅganīvīṃ suśroṇīṃ śyāmāṃ kanakamekhalām pad‍bhyāṃ kvaṇad‍bhyāṃ calantīṃ nūpurairdevatāmiva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: She put on a reddish-brown garment round her waist with a girdle of gold around it. The dark-complexioned[11] lady with well formed hips walked with her feet making a tinkling sound with the adorning anklets.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes the following from the Chānd. Up. to explain the darkness of complexion: annamayaṃ hi somya mana itiyat kṛṣṇam tad annasya /

devanagari: स्तनौ व्यञ्जितकैशोरौ समवृत्तौ निरन्तरौ । वस्त्रान्तेन निगूहन्तीं व्रीडया गजगामिनीम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: stanau vyañjitakaiśorau samavṛttau nirantarau vastrāntena nigūhantīṃ vrīḍayā gajagāminīm
translation: Out of bashfulness, she was (now and then) covering with the end of her garments, her symmetrical, closely knit, round (spherical) breasts indicating the advent of youth. She was walking (in the stately gait) like an elephant.

devanagari: तामाह ललितं वीरः सव्रीडस्मितशोभनाम् । स्निग्धेनापाङ्गपुङ्खेन स्पृष्टः प्रेमोद्‍भ्रमद्‍भ्रुवा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmāha lalitaṃ vīraḥ savrīḍasmitaśobhanām snigdhenāpāṅgapuṅkhena spṛṣṭaḥ premod‍bhramad‍bhruvā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ lal ]
[nominative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ lal ], [accusative single from √ lal ]
[instrumental single from √ snih class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ snih class 4 verb]
[instrumental single from √ snih class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ snih class 4 verb]

translation: Being (as if) wounded with the arrows of her charming side-long glances, shot from the bow of her eyebrows making lively amorous movements, the hero (Purañjana) spoke in soft winsome tones to the girl who looked all the more lovely by her bashful smiles.

devanagari: का त्वं कञ्जपलाशाक्षि कस्यासीह कुतः सति । इमामुप पुरीं भीरु किं चिकीर्षसि शंस मे ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: kā tvaṃ kañjapalāśākṣi kasyāsīha kutaḥ sati imāmupa purīṃ bhīru kiṃ cikīrṣasi śaṃsa me
translation: “Who are you, Oh beautiful lady with eyes like the lotus-petals? Whose (relative) are you? Whence have you come here? Oh timid lady, please tell me what you desire to do here on the outskirts of the city?

devanagari: क एतेऽनुपथा ये ते एकादश महाभटाः । एता वा ललनाः सुभ्रु कोऽयं तेऽहिः पुरःसरः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ka ete etā vā lalanāḥ subhru ko
translation: Who are these ten attendants commanded by the formidable warrior, forming the eleventh? Who are these beautiful damsels? Who is the serpent who goes ahead of you, Oh lady with beautiful eyebrows?

devanagari: त्वं ह्रीर्भवान्यस्यथ वाग्रमा पतिं । विचिन्वती किं मुनिवद्रहो वने । त्वदङ्घ्रिकामाप्तसमस्तकामं । क्व पद्मकोशः पतितः कराग्रात् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ hrīrbhavānyasyatha vāgramā patiṃ vicinvatī kiṃ munivadraho vane tvadaṅghrikāmāptasamastakāmaṃ kva padmakośaḥ patitaḥ karāgrāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]

translation: Are you the goddess Hrī, Pārvatī or Sarasvatī (the goddess of speech) or the goddess Lakṣmī leading the life of a sage in this secluded forest seeking a husband who must have surely got all his wishes fulfilled as your feet are desired by him?[12] If you be the goddess Lakṣmī, where has the lotus been dropped from your hand?
purport:
Footnote [12]:
According to most commentators: But the alternate explanation “who must... wishes fulfilled as his feet are desired by you” is worth considering.

devanagari: नासां वरोर्वन्यतमा भुविस्पृक् । पुरीं इमां वीरवरेण साकम् । अर्हस्यलङ्कर्तुमदभ्रकर्मणा । लोकं परं श्रीरिव यज्ञपुंसा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: nāsāṃ varorvanyatamā bhuvispṛk purīṃ imāṃ vīravareṇa sākam arhasyalaṅkartumadabhrakarmaṇā lokaṃ paraṃ śrīriva yajñapuṃsā
translation: Oh lady with beautiful thighs, you are surely none of these deities as you touch the earth (with your feet). You should, therefore, adorn this city along with me (who am) an excellent warrior of great exploits, just as goddess Lakṣmī as the consort of Lord Viṣṇu (the Lord of Sacrifices) graces Vai- kuṇṭha (the highest region).

devanagari: यदेष मापाङ्गविखण्डितेन्द्रियं । सव्रीडभावस्मितविभ्रमद्‍भ्रुवा । त्वयोपसृष्टो भगवान् मनोभवः । प्रबाधतेऽथानुगृहाण शोभने ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yadeṣa māpāṅgavikhaṇḍitendriyaṃ savrīḍabhāvasmitavibhramad‍bhruvā tvayopasṛṣṭo bhagavān manobhavaḥ prabādhate
translation: Please show favour to me, Oh graceful lady, as the mind-born god of love, prompted by you with the playful movement of your eyebrows and your bashful smile expressing love, is harassing me by piercing my heart with your sideglances.

devanagari: त्वदाननं सुभ्रु सुतारलोचनं । व्यालम्बिनीलालकवृन्दसंवृतम् । उन्नीय मे दर्शय वल्गुवाचकं । यद्व्रीडया नाभिमुखं शुचिस्मिते ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tvadānanaṃ subhru sutāralocanaṃ vyālambinīlālakavṛndasaṃvṛtam unnīya me darśaya valguvācakaṃ yadvrīḍayā nābhimukhaṃ śucismite

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh lady with bright smiles! Please raise up and show me your sweet speaking face with beautiful eyebrows and eyes of bright, charming pupils—your face which is covered with locks of long dark-blue hair—as it is not turned to me through bashfulness.”

devanagari: नारद उवाच । इत्थं पुरञ्जनं नारी याचमानमधीरवत् । अभ्यनन्दत तं वीरं हसन्ती वीर मोहिता ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvāca itthaṃ purañjanaṃ nārī yācamānamadhīravat abhyanandata taṃ vīraṃ hasantī vīra mohitā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ yāc class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yāc class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ru class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ has class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ has class 1 verb]

translation:
Nārada said:
Oh warrior! The lady was infatuated with Purañjana, the hero[13] who was courting her so impatiently and (in response) greeted him with a smile
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā presumably reads vīkṣya and interprets dṛṣṭvā (having seen).

devanagari: न विदाम वयं सम्यक् कर्तारं पुरुषर्षभ । आत्मनश्च परस्यापि गोत्रं नाम च यत्कृतम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: na vidāma vayaṃ samyak kartāraṃ puruṣarṣabha ātmanaśca parasyāpi gotraṃ nāma ca yatkṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: “Oh prominent man, we do not know properly who is your or my progenitor. No, do we know the family or the name (by which we are called?)

devanagari: इहाद्य सन्तमात्मानं विदाम न ततः परम् । येनेयं निर्मिता वीर पुरी शरणमात्मनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ihādya santamātmānaṃ vidāma na tataḥ param yeneyaṃ nirmitā vīra purī śaraṇamātmanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]

translation: We know that we are here today, now. We do not know beyond that. Nor do we know who created this city which is my abode, Oh warrior.

devanagari: एते सखायः सख्यो मे नरा नार्यश्च मानद । सुप्तायां मयि जागर्ति नागोऽयं पालयन् पुरीम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ete sakhāyaḥ sakhyo me narā nāryaśca mānada suptāyāṃ mayi jāgarti nāgo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pāl class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ pāl class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ pā ], [vocative single from √ pā ]

translation: Oh respectful one, these men and women, who are my friends, stay awake while I am asleep. So does this serpent guarding this city.

devanagari: दिष्ट्याऽगतोऽसि भद्रं ते ग्राम्यान् कामानभीप्ससे । उद्वहिष्यामि तांस्तेऽहं स्वबन्धुभिः अरिन्दम ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā udvahiṣyāmi tāṃste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Leave aside the details about my name, family etc. Oh destroyer of enemies, I am glad that you have come. God bless you! Whatever sensual pleasures you desire, I shall secure for you with the help of my kinsmen.

devanagari: इमां त्वं अधितिष्ठस्व पुरीं नवमुखीं विभो । मयोपनीतान् गृह्णानः कामभोगान् शतं समाः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: imāṃ tvaṃ adhitiṣṭhasva purīṃ navamukhīṃ vibho mayopanītān gṛhṇānaḥ kāmabhogān śataṃ samāḥ
translation: Please occupy this city of pine gates, enjoying for hundred years all the sense-pleasures which I shall secure for you, Oh Lord.

devanagari: कं नु त्वदन्यं रमये ह्यरतिज्ञमकोविदम् । असम्परायाभिमुखं अश्वस्तनविदं पशुम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃ nu tvadanyaṃ ramaye hyaratijñamakovidam asamparāyābhimukhaṃ aśvastanavidaṃ paśum
translation: For, whom else than you should I really please—him who is ignorant of erotics or pleasure, void of learning or wisdom, careless about tomorrow’s work (i.e. his life here) and heedless about his life hereafter, a sheer brute.

devanagari: धर्मो ह्यत्रार्थकामौ च प्रजानन्दोऽमृतं यशः । लोका विशोका विरजा यान्न केवलिनो विदुः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmo hyatrārthakāmau ca prajānando lokā viśokā virajā yānna kevalino viduḥ
translation: Here (in the life of a householder) one attains to puruṣārthas called dharma (righteousness), artha (wealth) and kāma (pleasures of the senses). It is here that one gets the joy of getting progeny, immortality (i.e. Liberation—Mokṣa), fame and regions which are free from sins, sorrow—which even recluses do not know.

devanagari: पितृदेवर्षिमर्त्यानां भूतानां आत्मनश्च ह । क्षेम्यं वदन्ति शरणं भवेऽस्मिन् यद्‍गृहाश्रमः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: pitṛdevarṣimartyānāṃ bhūtānāṃ ātmanaśca ha kṣemyaṃ vadanti śaraṇaṃ bhave

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: They, the sages of old, say that in this world, the stage of householder’s life is the only asylum for the well-being of manes (pitṛs), gods, sages, men and all other beings and of the householder himself.

devanagari: का नाम वीर विख्यातं वदान्यं प्रियदर्शनम् । न वृणीत प्रियं प्राप्तं मादृशी त्वादृशं पतिम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kā nāma vīra vikhyātaṃ vadānyaṃ priyadarśanam na vṛṇīta priyaṃ prāptaṃ mādṛśī tvādṛśaṃ patim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative middle third single], [imperative active second plural]
[optative middle third single], [imperative active second plural]

translation: Oh warrior, what girl in my position (lit. like me) would not accept as a husband a famous, munificent, goodlooking, loving personage like you.

devanagari: कस्या मनस्ते भुवि भोगिभोगयोः । स्त्रिया न सज्जेद्‍भुजयोर्महाभुज । योऽनाथवर्गाधिमलं घृणोद्धत । स्मितावलोकेन चरत्यपोहितुम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: kasyā manaste bhuvi bhogibhogayoḥ striyā na sajjed‍bhujayormahābhuja yo smitāvalokena caratyapohitum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ smi class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ smi class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ smi class 1 verb]

translation: Oh mighty-armed hero! What woman’s heart in this world would not cling to your arms which are (tender yet powerful) like the body of a serpent?—You move about in the world in order to remove the mental anguish of the helpless by your smiling looks full of compassion.”

devanagari: नारद उवाच । इति तौ दम्पती तत्र समुद्य समयं मिथः । तां प्रविश्य पुरीं राजन् मुमुदाते शतं समाः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvāca iti tau dampatī tatra samudya samayaṃ mithaḥ tāṃ praviśya purīṃ rājan mumudāte śataṃ samāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Nārada said:
Oh King (Prācīnabarhis)! In this way the couple entered into a mutual agreement there. They then entered the city and lived in happiness for a hundred years.

devanagari: उपगीयमानो ललितं तत्र तत्र च गायकैः । क्रीडन् परिवृतः स्त्रीभिः ह्रदिनीं आविशत् शुचौ ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: upagīyamāno lalitaṃ tatra tatra ca gāyakaiḥ krīḍan parivṛtaḥ strībhiḥ hradinīṃ āviśat śucau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[accusative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ lal ]
[nominative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ lal class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ lal ], [accusative single from √ lal ]
[nominative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]

translation: Sweetly eulogised by bards everywhere, and playing in company of women, he entered the (swimming-) pool in the hot season.

devanagari: सप्तोपरि कृता द्वारः पुरस्तस्यास्तु द्वे अधः । पृथग् विषयगत्यर्थं तस्यां यः कश्चनेश्वरः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: saptopari kṛtā dvāraḥ purastasyāstu dve adhaḥ pṛthag viṣayagatyarthaṃ tasyāṃ yaḥ kaścaneśvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: For (the convenience of) the movements from and to the outer regions, of whosoever (unknown) was the master of the city there were seven gateways in the upper (i.e. front-) part of the city (viz. two eyes, two ears, two nostrils and a mouth) and two in the lower part (the anus and the penis).

devanagari: पञ्च द्वारस्तु पौरस्त्या दक्षिणैका तथोत्तरा । पश्चिमे द्वे अमूषां ते नामानि नृप वर्णये ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: pañca dvārastu paurastyā dakṣiṇaikā tathottarā paścime dve amūṣāṃ te nāmāni nṛpa varṇaye
translation: There were five doors to the east (front, viz., two eyes, two nostrils and one mouth), one to the South (the right ear), one to the North (the left ear) and two to the west (viz. the penis and the anus). I shall describe to you the names of these, Oh King.

devanagari: खद्योताऽविर्मुखी च प्राग् द्वारावेकत्र निर्मिते । विभ्राजितं जनपदं याति ताभ्यां द्युमत्सखः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: khadyotā vibhrājitaṃ janapadaṃ yāti tābhyāṃ dyumatsakhaḥ
translation: [14] To the East were constructed adjacently a pair of gates called khadyotā (fire-fly) and Avirmukhī (of manifest face—bright light). Through them he (king Purañjana) accompanied by his friend Dyumat, used to visit the country called Vibhrājit.

devanagari: नलिनी नालिनी च प्राग् द्वारौ एकत्र निर्मिते । अवधूतसखस्ताभ्यां विषयं याति सौरभम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: nalinī nālinī ca prāg dvārau ekatra nirmite avadhūtasakhastābhyāṃ viṣayaṃ yāti saurabham
translation: [15] A pair of adjacent gates called Nalinī and Nālinī were also built to the east. Through them he (Purañjana) in company of Avadhūta, goes to the region called Saurabha (Fragrance).

devanagari: मुख्या नाम पुरस्ताद् द्वाः तयाऽपणबहूदनौ । विषयौ याति पुरराड् रसज्ञविपणान्वितः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: mukhyā nāma purastād dvāḥ tayā viṣayau yāti purarāḍ rasajñavipaṇānvitaḥ
translation: [16] The fifth gate to the east was called Mukhyā (the main). In association with Rasajña and Vipaṇa, the king of the city goes through the gate to the countries of Āpaṇa and Bahūdana.
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Herein Āpaṇa signifies speech, and Bahūdana denotes variety of food. Vipaṇa thus stands for the organ of speech and Rasajña, for the capacity of the tongue to taste—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: पितृहूर्नृप पुर्या द्वाः दक्षिणेन पुरञ्जनः । राष्ट्रं दक्षिणपञ्चालं याति श्रुतधरान्वितः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: pitṛhūrnṛpa puryā dvāḥ dakṣiṇena purañjanaḥ rāṣṭraṃ dakṣiṇapañcālaṃ yāti śrutadharānvitaḥ
translation: [17] On King (Barhiṣad)! Through the southern gate of the city called Pitṛhū, Purañjana, in accompaniment of Śrutadhara used to go to the country called Southern Pañcāla.
purport:
Footnote [17]:
The right ear is the Southern gate leading to the region of Pitṛs by the path of ritualistic karmas. It hears the karma-kāṇḍa, the path of the karmas. Due to its association with Pitṛ-yāṇa the right ear is called Pitṛhū. Pañcāla here is the science of Pravṛtti mārga.

devanagari: देवहूर्नाम पुर्या द्वा उत्तरेण पुरञ्जनः । राष्ट्रं उत्तरपञ्चालं याति श्रुतधरान्वितः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: devahūrnāma puryā dvā uttareṇa purañjanaḥ rāṣṭraṃ uttarapañcālaṃ yāti śrutadharānvitaḥ
translation: [18] By the northern gate of the city, known as Devahū, Purañjana along with Śrutadhara, used to go to the country called Northern Pañcāla.
purport:
Footnote [18]:
Devahū is the left ear. It leads to the region of gods by following the Devayāna Path.

devanagari: आसुरी नाम पश्चाद् द्वाः तया याति पुरञ्जनः । ग्रामकं नाम विषयं दुर्मदेन समन्वितः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: āsurī nāma paścād dvāḥ tayā yāti purañjanaḥ grāmakaṃ nāma viṣayaṃ durmadena samanvitaḥ
translation: To the west of the city was the gate called Āsurī (pertaining to asuras—people indulging in sensual pleasures). In company of Durmada, Purañjana used to go to the country called Grāmaka (low or vulgar pleasures).

devanagari: निर्ऋतिर्नाम पश्चाद् द्वाः तया याति पुरञ्जनः । वैशसं नाम विषयं लुब्धकेन समन्वितः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: nirṛtirnāma paścād dvāḥ tayā yāti purañjanaḥ vaiśasaṃ nāma viṣayaṃ lubdhakena samanvitaḥ
translation: There was another gate to the West. It was called Nvṛti. Through it Purañjana went to the country called Vaiśasa (terrible), in company of Lubdhaka (a miser).

devanagari: अन्धावमीषां पौराणां निर्वाक् पेशस्कृतावुभौ । अक्षण्वतामधिपतिः ताभ्यां याति करोति च ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: andhāvamīṣāṃ paurāṇāṃ nirvāk peśaskṛtāvubhau akṣaṇvatāmadhipatiḥ tābhyāṃ yāti karoti ca
translation: Out of the citizens (there were) two viz. Nirvāk [Nirvāc?] (speechless) and Peśaskṛta (possessing no aperture). With these the lord of senses made movements of walking and doing.

devanagari: स यर्ह्यन्तःपुरगतो विषूचीनसमन्वितः । मोहं प्रसादं हर्षं वा याति जायात्मजोद्‍भवम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yarhyantaḥpuragato viṣūcīnasamanvitaḥ mohaṃ prasādaṃ harṣaṃ vā yāti jāyātmajod‍bhavam
translation: If he goes to his harem (viz. heart) along with (his friends) Viṣūcīna (the chief of attendant in a harem), he experiences delusion, calmness and (excitement of) joy, from wife and children.

devanagari: एवं कर्मसु संसक्तः कामात्मा वञ्चितोऽबुधः । महिषी यद् यद् ईहेत तत्तद् एवान्ववर्तत ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ karmasu saṃsaktaḥ kāmātmā vañcito mahiṣī yad yad īheta tattad evānvavartata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The unwise king (Purañjana) who was thus deeply attached to karmas (action) and whose mind was completely given up to sensual pleasures, was enraptured and whatever the queen wished to do, he acted accordingly.

devanagari: क्वचित्पिबन्त्यां पिबति मदिरां मदविह्वलः । अश्नन्त्यां क्वचिदश्नाति जक्षत्यां सह जक्षिति ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacitpibantyāṃ pibati madirāṃ madavihvalaḥ aśnantyāṃ kvacidaśnāti jakṣatyāṃ saha jakṣiti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

translation: Sometimes when she used to drink wine, he used to drink and get intoxicated. Sometimes when she took meals, he took it with her; when she chewed, he did the same.

devanagari: क्वचिद्‍गायति गायन्त्यां रुदत्यां रुदति क्वचित् । क्वचिद् हसन्त्यां हसति जल्पन्त्यामनु जल्पति ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid‍gāyati gāyantyāṃ rudatyāṃ rudati kvacit kvacid hasantyāṃ hasati jalpantyāmanu jalpati
translation: Sometimes when she sang, he used to sing; sometimes he wept when she shed tears; sometimes he followed her while she laughed; sometimes when she chattered, he prattled.

devanagari: क्वचिद् धावति धावन्त्यां तिष्ठन्त्यामनु तिष्ठति । अनु शेते शयानायां अन्वास्ते क्वचिदासतीम् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacid dhāvati dhāvantyāṃ tiṣṭhantyāmanu tiṣṭhati anu śete śayānāyāṃ anvāste kvacidāsatīm
translation: Sometimes when she ran, he ran; he stood when she stood. When she lay in bed, he did so; sometimes when she sat up, he also sat up.

devanagari: क्वचित् श्रृणोति श्रृण्वन्त्यां पश्यन्त्यामनु पश्यति । क्वचित् जिघ्रति जिघ्रन्त्यां स्पृशन्त्यां स्पृशति क्वचित् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: kvacit śrṛṇoti śrṛṇvantyāṃ paśyantyāmanu paśyati kvacit jighrati jighrantyāṃ spṛśantyāṃ spṛśati kvacit

[locative single from √ ghrā class 1 verb], [locative single from √ ghrā class 3 verb]

[nominative plural from √ ghrā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ghrā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ghrā class 1 verb], [locative single from √ ghrā class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ ghrā class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ ghrā class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ ghrā class 3 verb], [locative single from √ ghrā class 3 verb]

translation: Occasionally, when she heard, he heard; when she saw, he also saw it; sometimes she smelt, he also smelt; when she touched, he used to touch.

devanagari: क्वचिच्च शोचतीं जायां अनु शोचति दीनवत् । अनु हृष्यति हृष्यन्त्यां मुदितामनु मोदते ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacicca śocatīṃ jāyāṃ anu śocati dīnavat anu hṛṣyati hṛṣyantyāṃ muditāmanu modate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śuc class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ śuc class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ śuc class 1 verb], [locative single from √ śuc class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ nu class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nu class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nu class 1 verb]

translation: Sometimes when the wife cried in sorrow, he wept after her like a man in distress; when she rejoiced, he rejoiced; on her being delighted, he was delighted.

devanagari: विप्रलब्धो महिष्यैवं सर्वप्रकृतिवञ्चितः । नेच्छन् अनुकरोत्यज्ञः क्लैब्यात् क्रीडामृगो यथा ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: vipralabdho mahiṣyaivaṃ sarvaprakṛtivañcitaḥ necchan anukarotyajñaḥ klaibyāt krīḍāmṛgo yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation:
In this way, he was deceived by the queen and lost his original nature (such as non-attachment to everything). The ignorant King though unwilling, simply imitated (the acts of his queen) helplessly like a domesticated monkey kept for amusement.
